summary: Raj facilitated a team standup with Ravi and Sofia covering post-mortem follow-ups from the Detect outage and the Comply v2 rollout status. Sofia identified a critical bug in Comply v2 causing SOC 2 report generation timeouts for enterprise customers with large tenant datasets, with a fix targeting early next week. The team reviewed outstanding Detect post-mortem action items, including a circuit breaker PR that needs a health check added before merging and a load testing suite formally scheduled to begin April 28th. The runbook update was also flagged as incomplete and assigned to Sofia to finish this week.
actionItems: Sofia Petrov: Develop and submit PR for Comply v2 audit log pagination fix by end of day tomorrow, targeting production deployment early next week
Ravi Gupta: Add health check probe to circuit breaker PR and ping Sofia for review by end of day
Sofia Petrov: Complete the two outstanding detection pipeline sections of the post-mortem runbook this week
Ravi Gupta: Add load testing suite to Jira roadmap with April 28th start date and two-week sprint plan

keyMoments.text: Sofia identifies a timeout bug in Comply v2 affecting enterprise customers with large tenant datasets across all compliance frameworks, noting it is not an edge case
keyMoments.speaker: Sofia Petrov

keyMoments.text: Load testing suite identified as not yet started despite being a post-mortem commitment; Raj pushes back on letting it slip and formalizes it on the roadmap
keyMoments.speaker: Raj Kapoor

keyMoments.text: Sofia raises a safety concern about the circuit breaker failover logic routing blindly to potentially degraded secondary nodes without a health check
keyMoments.speaker: Sofia Petrov

keyMoments.text: Raj sides with Sofia to include the health check in the current PR rather than deferring it, ensuring the circuit breaker pattern ships correctly
keyMoments.speaker: Raj Kapoor
